column1: # whittle ONT adapter/primer/barcode catalog — our own, assembled from primary sources (2026-07-06)
# Columns: id <TAB> category <TAB> end <TAB> sequence <TAB> kits <TAB> source
#   end: 5 = front/read-start, 3 = rear/read-end, both = search at either end
#        (every sequence is also searched reverse-complemented, so opposite-strand reads are covered)
#   category: ligation-adapter | rapid-adapter | rna-adapter | primer | flank | barcode
#   qcat kit YAMLs. Barcodes BC25/50/96 (Porechop) confirmed present in dorado. Sequences are ONT-
#   published facts; catalog assembled independently for whittle. See spec: adapter-trimming design.
# Barcode DEDUP rule: a barcode number is ONE shared 24 bp oligo across native/PCR/rapid kits; the
# families differ only by FLANK + orientation (native uses the reverse-complement). We store the
# canonical FORWARD oligo once (BC01..BC96) and rely on whittle's built-in reverse-complement search
# for the native orientation. Trimming through the flanks removes the barcode regardless of number.
# --- Ligation Y-adapter (two chemistry generations; keep BOTH) ---
# --- Rapid adapter (also the 3' flank of rapid-barcoding kits) ---
# --- Barcode flanks (dorado kit-14 constants; trimming through a flank removes the barcode) ---
# --- Barcodes (96 canonical forward oligos; searched + reverse-complemented at both ends) ---
